{{Short description="Vector quantity determining the torque on a magnetic dipole in a magnetic field}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Magnetic moment
| image = Magnetic moment.svg
| caption = A current loop generates a magnetic dipole — the source of a magnetic moment.
| topics = [[Magnetic field]], [[Spin (physics)]], [[Electric current]], [[Electromagnetism]]
| symbol = μ, m
| SI_unit = J/T or A·m²
}}

The '''magnetic moment''' (or '''magnetic dipole moment''') is a [[vector (physics)|vector]] quantity that determines the [[torque]] a magnetic dipole experiences in an external [[magnetic field]], and the strength of the magnetic field the dipole itself produces.

== Definition ==
For a planar current loop carrying current <math>I</math> and enclosing area <math>A</math>:
:<math>\boldsymbol{\mu} = I\mathbf{A}</math>

where <math>\mathbf{A}</math> is the vector area (direction given by right-hand rule).

== Torque on a magnetic dipole ==
A magnetic dipole in an external field <math>\mathbf{B}</math> experiences a torque:
:<math>\boldsymbol{\tau} = \boldsymbol{\mu} \times \mathbf{B}</math>

Potential energy:
:<math>U = -\boldsymbol{\mu} \cdot \mathbf{B}</math>

== Orbital magnetic moment ==
For a charged particle in a circular orbit:
:<math>\boldsymbol{\mu}_L = -\frac{e}{2m_e}\mathbf{L}</math>
where <math>\mathbf{L}</math> is the orbital [[angular momentum]].

== Spin magnetic moment ==
The intrinsic magnetic moment associated with [[spin (physics)|spin]]:
:<math>\boldsymbol{\mu}_S = -g\frac{e}{2m_e}\mathbf{S}</math>

where <math>g \approx 2.0023</math> is the electron [[g-factor|spin g-factor]]. The [[anomalous magnetic dipole moment]] (deviation from <math>g=2</math>) is one of the most precisely verified predictions of [[quantum electrodynamics]].

=== Bohr magneton ===
The [[Bohr magneton]] is the natural unit of magnetic moment for electrons:
:<math>\mu_B = \frac{e\hbar}{2m_e} = 9.274 \times 10^{-24}\ \text{J/T}</math>

=== Nuclear magneton ===
For nucleons:
:<math>\mu_N = \frac{e\hbar}{2m_p} = 5.051 \times 10^{-27}\ \text{J/T}</math>

== Applications ==
* [[MRI]] — detection of proton magnetic moments in tissues
* [[NMR spectroscopy]] — chemical structure determination
* [[Data storage]] — magnetic hard drives
* [[Ferromagnetism]] — alignment of atomic magnetic moments
* [[Magnetic nanoparticle]]s — biomedical applications

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Angular momentum]]
* [[Magnetic field]]
* [[Spin (physics)]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Magnetic moment| ]]
[[Category:Electromagnetism]]
[[Category:Magnetism]]
